summary: Ukrainian forces reported a mass drone attack on Kharkiv causing six civilian injuries and a residential fire, while Russian missile strikes hit Cheboksary and the Samara oil refinery. Ukraine claimed to have destroyed the Mariupol port infrastructure and the shadow‑fleet vessel Lady Augusta, severely limiting Russian logistics. Both sides reported intensified UAV activity, with Ukraine saying Russia launches 650 drones and missiles daily.
key_points: Mass drone attack on Kharkiv injured at least six civilians and ignited a fire in an industrial district building (Source: @UkraineNow/81820).
Russian missile strike hit Cheboksary, 1,000 km from the front, and the Novokuybyshevsk oil refinery in Samara caught fire (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Ukrainian forces destroyed Mariupol port infrastructure, including power substations, radar, a control tower, and the shadow‑fleet cargo ship Lady Augusta (Source: @UkraineNow/81817).
Russia attempted a missile strike on the Genichesk‑Arabat Spit bridge in Kherson, temporarily suspending traffic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Eight districts experienced power outages after a nighttime Ukrainian UAV attack (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Ukrainian officials reported Russia launches 650 drones and 35‑100 missiles per day at Ukraine (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30802).
Ukrainian military warned of new Russian “Molnia” drones operating on video frequencies hard to detect (Source: @UkraineNow/81812).
Russian FPV drones wounded civilians in Belgorod region villages, including severe injuries in Krasnaya Yaruga (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).

sections.executive_summary: Mass drone attacks on Kharkiv injured six civilians and sparked a residential fire. Russian missile strikes hit Cheboksary and set the Samara oil refinery ablaze. Ukraine reported the destruction of Mariupol port facilities and the shadow‑fleet vessel Lady Augusta, crippling Russian logistics.

points: Kharkiv endured a large‑scale UAV assault, with at least six civilians injured, stress reactions reported, and a fire breaking out in a residential building in the industrial district (Source: @UkraineNow/81820).
In Odessa’s Primorsky district, Russian attacks damaged two residential buildings, completely destroying a fifth‑floor apartment and hospitalising a 46‑year‑old woman (Source: @UkraineNow/81809).

Ukrainian forces eliminated key Mariupol port assets, including power substations, radar, a control tower, repair facilities, fuel tanks, and the shadow‑fleet cargo ship Lady Augusta, rendering the port non‑operational (Source: @UkraineNow/81817).
Ukraine’s defense ministry warned that Russian “Molnia” drones now operate on video frequencies that evade most electronic detectors, urging units to adapt (Source: @UkraineNow/81812).
Ukrainian officials stated Russia is launching approximately 650 drones and 35‑100 missiles daily against Ukraine, while Ukraine can field 300‑350 defensive systems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30802).

Ukrainian intelligence reported a group of three army‑corps officers in Kharkiv region suspected of extorting soldiers for false combat reports, involving 274 k UAH in illicit gains (Source: @UkraineNow/81815).
A Ukrainian FPV drone strike wounded a man in the Graivoron district of Belgorod region, demonstrating cross‑border UAV activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
A missile attack struck Cheboksary in Chuvashia, located about 1,000 km from the front lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
The Novokuybyshevsk oil refinery in Samara region caught fire after Ukrainian strikes, causing significant industrial damage (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Russian forces attempted a missile strike on the bridge linking Genichesk to the Arabat Spit in Kherson region, temporarily halting traffic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Eight districts suffered power outages following a nighttime Ukrainian UAV attack on energy infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Russian FPV drones caused severe civilian injuries in Belgorod region villages, including life‑threatening wounds in Krasnaya Yaruga (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Russian troops consolidated around Kurylovka in the Kupyansk direction and continued small‑group attacks toward Kupiansk‑Vuzlovy (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
In the Dobropillya direction, Russian forces advanced northwest from Myrne‑Vasylevka and west of Rodynske, maintaining pressure on Ukrainian positions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Ukrainian assault units of Army Group “North” engaged in small‑arms battles around Kazacha Lopan, a major logistics hub in Kharkiv region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Fighting continued in Vovchansk direction, with Ukrainian forces clashing in the villages of Ohrimivka and Losivka and surrounding forest areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
In Sumy’s Shostka district, Ukrainian troops advanced up to 900 m in 21 sectors, with ongoing combat in Ivolzhanske, Pysarovka, and Novaya Sich (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Combat persisted in Krasnopolye district forests near Mihaylovka and along railway tracks to the district centre (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Ukrainian forces reported small‑arms engagements in the Zaporozhye front, with continual attacks on electrical substations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Russian forces in the Kupyansk direction consolidated near Kurylovka while probing towards Kupiansk‑Vuzlovy (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Ukrainian units in the Velyky Burluk direction fought in forested areas near Petro‑Ivanovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported near Cheboksary, approximately 1,000 km from the front, indicating long‑range reconnaissance or strike capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).

No direct combat reports were provided for the northern front in the source set; however, Russian missile strikes on Cheboksary and Samara suggest strategic targeting from the north (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Ukrainian intelligence noted the presence of reactive UAVs operating from the north of Kharkiv heading southeast, indicating potential threat vectors (Source: @kpszsu/64724).
Reports of a reactive UAV originating from the north of Kharkiv moving southeast underscore continued aerial pressure on northern sectors (Source: @kpszsu/64724).
No further specific northern front engagements were detailed in the provided messages.
Ukrainian forces destroyed Mariupol port infrastructure and the shadow‑fleet vessel Lady Augusta, severely limiting Russian logistical use of the port (Source: @UkraineNow/81817).
Russia attempted a missile strike on the Genichesk‑Arabat Spit bridge in Kherson, temporarily halting civilian traffic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Four wounded civilians were reported in Kherson region locations: Skadovsk, Velyka Kardashynka, Velyky Kopany, and Kalanchak (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Ukrainian reports indicated a group of reactive UAVs launched from the Black Sea heading toward Kherson region (Source: @kpszsu/64721).
Ukrainian officials warned of Russian “Molnia” drones operating on hard‑to‑detect video frequencies, affecting southern defenses (Source: @UkraineNow/81812).

Ukrainian intelligence noted possible preparations for a ballistic missile launch of medium range “Oreshnyk” (RS‑26) at the Kapustin Yar test site, though its intended target remains unclear (Source: @eRadarrua/96124).
Ukraine reported that Russia is launching approximately 650 drones and 35‑100 missiles per day against Ukrainian targets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30802).
Eight UAVs heading for Moscow were destroyed, according to Ukrainian operational reports (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Ukrainian forces observed Russian FPV drones causing civilian injuries in Belgorod region villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Ukrainian officials highlighted the emergence of Russian “Molnia” drones using video frequencies that evade most electronic detection systems (Source: @UkraineNow/81812).
Multiple UAV launches were reported across Ukrainian regions, including guided aviation bombs over Kharkiv, and reactive UAVs from the Black Sea toward Kherson (Source: @kpszsu/64717; @kpszsu/64721).
Ukrainian reports indicated UAV activity over the industrial district of Kharkiv and over the city of Cheboksary, suggesting expanded aerial reach (Source: @UkraineNow/81820; @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Russian missile strikes targeted energy infrastructure in Zaporozhye region, causing damage during a UAV raid (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).

key_developments: A mass UAV assault on Kharkiv injured six civilians and ignited a fire in an industrial district residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/81820).
Russian missile strikes hit Cheboksary, 1,000 km from the front, and set the Novokuybyshevsk oil refinery in Samara ablaze (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Ukrainian forces destroyed Mariupol port infrastructure and the shadow‑fleet cargo ship Lady Augusta, rendering the port inoperable (Source: @UkraineNow/81817).
Russia attempted a missile strike on the Genichesk‑Arabat Spit bridge in Kherson, temporarily suspending traffic (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Eight districts experienced power outages after a nighttime Ukrainian UAV attack on energy facilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
Ukraine claims Russia launches 650 drones and 35‑100 missiles daily, while Ukraine can field 300‑350 defensive systems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30802).
Russian FPV drones wounded civilians in Belgorod region villages, including severe injuries in Krasnaya Yaruga (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30805).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV and missile activity remains high, with long‑range strikes on Cheboksary and Samara indicating strategic targeting beyond the immediate front. Ukraine's destruction of Mariupol port assets and the shadow‑fleet vessel significantly degrades Russian logistical capabilities in the south. The reported introduction of Russian “Molnia” drones operating on hard‑to‑detect frequencies poses a new challenge for Ukrainian air defenses, while the high volume of Russian drone and missile launches stresses Ukrainian interception capacity.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are tightening sanctions, with the EU proposing bans on all Russian participants in the war and new measures against Russian energy and fisheries sectors. Ukraine continues to seek international support for defensive aid, highlighting the need for advanced air‑defense systems to counter evolving UAV threats. Regional actors such as Poland and Germany are adjusting policy stances amid broader geopolitical tensions linked to the conflict.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian intelligence reports indicate preparation for possible medium‑range ballistic missile launches from Kapustin Yar, though target intentions remain unclear. Increased UAV activity from the Black Sea toward Kherson suggests a focus on southern supply lines. Continued Russian FPV drone incursions into Belgorod region highlight cross‑border threats to civilian populations, requiring heightened border surveillance.
